zh.lifeSummary: 當Grace Porter於1595年1月8日出生在英國藍卡爾吉可哈姆時，她的父親John Porter31歲，母親Sybil Vessey33歲。她於1618年11月17日與William Judson在英國越可爾哈利法可結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1659年9月29日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文紐哈文 堂享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Grace Porter于1595年1月8日出生在英国藍卡爾吉可哈姆时，她的父亲John Porter31岁，母亲Sybil Vessey33岁。她于1618年11月17日与William Judson在英国越可爾哈利法可结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1659年9月29日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 哈文紐哈文 堂享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: Porter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, castle, or monastery, from Middle English and Older Scots porter(e), port(o)ur ‘doorkeeper, gatekeeper’ (Anglo-Norman French port(i)er, portur, Latin portarius). The office often came with accommodation, lands, and other privileges for the bearer, and in some cases was hereditary, especially in the case of a royal castle. The name has been established in Ireland since the 13th century. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates and equivalents in other languages, for example German Pförtner (see Fortner ) and Poertner .

English: occupational name for a man who carried loads for a living, especially one who used his own muscle power rather than a beast of burden or a wheeled vehicle. This sense is from Middle English port(o)ur, porter ‘porter, carrier of burdens’ (Anglo-Norman French portur, porteo(u)r).
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized, of Poorter, status name for a freeman (burgher) of a town, Middle Dutch portere, modern Dutch poorter. Compare De Porter .

variants.name: Porterfield
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English porter(e) ‘gatekeeper, doorkeeper’ + feld ‘open country, bounded piece of land’, referring to the hereditary lands owned by a porter of a monastery (see Porter 1), taken as a surname by the descendants of such an official. Black suggests that they would originally have been known as Porter but extended their name when ‘territorial surnames’ became fashionable.

variants.name: Sluiter
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a porter or gatekeeper, Middle Dutch sluter. Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Port
meanings: English: from Middle English port(e) ‘gateway, entrance’ (Old French porte, from Latin porta), hence a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town or city, or for the gatekeeper. Compare Porter 1.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a harbor or in a market town, from a homonymous Middle English port(e) (Old English port ‘harbor, market town’, from Latin portus ‘harbor, haven’, reinforced in Middle English by Old French port, from the same source).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Port‐en‐Bessin (Calvados), which is recorded as Portu in 1035–36. The placename derives from Latin portus ‘port, harbour, haven’.

variants.name: Fortner
meanings: German: variant (also Förtner) or Americanized form of Pförtner, an occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper in a great house, castle, or monastery, Middle High German phortenaere (from Latin porta ‘door, gate’). Compare English Porter .

variants.name: Portmann
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Helmut, Franz, Hansi, Kurt.

North German: occupational name for a gatekeeper, from Middle Low German port(e) ‘gate’ (from Latin porta) + man ‘man’, or a topographic name for someone who lived near the gates of a fortified town. Compare Porter .

variants.name: Portier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Clovis, Leonce, Renauld.
French: occupational name for the gatekeeper of a walled town or city, or the doorkeeper of a great house, Old French portier (from Late Latin portarius). Compare English Porter .
History: Alexandre-Bernard Portier from Paris, France, married Catherine Monte in Edgard, LA, in 1741.

nameMeanings.name: Grace
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the abstract noun (via Old French, from Latin gratia), this name occurs occasionally in the 15th century, and by the 1540s was among the most popular girls' names in some parishes. It has always been particularly popular in Scotland and northern England (borne, for example, by Grace Darling , the lighthouse keeper's daughter whose heroism in 1838 , saving sailors in a storm, caught the popular imagination). In more recent times it was famous as the name of the actress Grace Kelly ( 1928–82 ), who became Princess Grace of Monaco. In Ireland it is used as an Anglicized form of Gráinne .

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."
On April 24, 1638, a company of five-hundred English Puritans led by the Reverend John Davenport and Theophilus Eaton, a wealthy London merchant, sailed into the harbor.

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Grace Porter was born on 8 January 1595, in Kirkham, Lancashire, England, her father, John Porter, was 31 and her mother, Sybil Vessey, was 33. She married William Judson on 17 November 1618, in Halifax, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 29 September 1659, in Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 64.

fr.New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut Colony: Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Grace Porter est née le 8 janvier 1595, à Kirkham, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, John Porter, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Sybil Vessey, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé William Judson le 17 novembre 1618, à Halifax, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 29 septembre 1659, à Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 64 ans.

ko.New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut Colony: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 뉴하븐 타운
ko.lifeSummary: Grace Porter(이)가 1595년 1월 8일 영국 랭카실르 커카에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Porter(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sybil Vessey(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1618년 11월 17일 영국 요르크실르 할이파스에서 William Judson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1659년 9월 29일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 하븐 뉴하븐 타운에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Grace Porter nasceu em 8 de janeiro de 1595, em Kirkham, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Porter, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Sybil Vessey, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com William Judson em 17 de novembro de 1618, em Halifax, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. ru.New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut Colony: нюхавен тоун, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Grace Porter родилась 8 января 1595 г. в местности кирхам, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Porter было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Sybil Vessey, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Judson 17 ноября 1618 г. в местности хэлифакс, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 29 сентября 1659 г. в местности нюхавен тоун, ню хавен, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 64 лет.
